title: Public Testimony on Park Street Pedestrian Deaths and Sidewalk Infrastructure Gaps
summary: Neighborhood activist John Meyer (Washington Square Neighborhood Watch/Task Force, 5154 Park St) delivers public testimony on pedestrian dangers. Reports 3 deaths in 3 years on Park Street and describes new Americans from nearby mosque walking in roads to reach the regional market. Recounts incident of woman losing groceries when struck by car at unfinished crosswalk. Meyer has published a 10-year goal of walkable access to the regional market. Engineer confirms project will install 2 crosswalks across Park Street, ADA corners with push buttons, and 10-foot shared-use paths connecting to DPW's Washington Square path. Additional questions about bike racks, Veo stations, and bus shelters — funding limited but locations can be designed into plans. Council also raises vehicle parking on shared-use paths as enforcement concern.
